content:
# 1 John Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1JN_003_page_001.jpg

1. Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits [to see] whether they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.

2. By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses Yeshua Christos having come in [the] flesh is from God,

3. and every spirit that does not confess Yeshua is not from God. And this is the [spirit] of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming, and now it is already in the world.

4. You are from God, little children, and have overcome them, because greater is the [one] in you than the [one] in the world.

5. They are from the world; because of this they speak from the world, and the world listens to them.

6. We are from God. The [one] knowing God listens to us; the [one] who is not from God does not listen to us. From this we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.

7. Beloved, let us love one another, because love is from God, and everyone who loves has been born of God and knows God.

8. The [one] not loving has not known God, because God is love.

9. By this the love of God was manifested among us, that God sent his only-begotten Son into the world so that we might live through him.

10. In this is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son [as] propitiation for our sins.

11. Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.

12. No one has ever beheld God. If we love one another, God abides in us and his love has been perfected in us.

13. By this we know that we abide in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.

14. And we have beheld and bear witness that the Father has sent the Son [as] Savior of the world.

15. Whoever confesses that Yeshua is the Son of God, God abides in him and he in God.

16. And we have known and believed the love which God has in us. God is love, and the [one] abiding in love abides in God, and God abides in him.

17. By this love has been perfected with us, so that we may have boldness in the day of judgment, because just as that [one] is, so also are we in this world.

18. There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear, because fear has punishment. And the [one] fearing has not been perfected in love.

19. We love because he first loved us.

20. If anyone says, "I love God," and hates his brother, he is a liar; for the [one] not loving his brother whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen.

21. And this commandment we have from him, that the [one] loving God should love his brother also.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "God is love":** ho theos agapē estin — the second Johannine "God is" declaration (cf. 1:5 "God is light"). No article before agapē — love is God's essential nature.

2. **v.10 — "propitiation":** hilasmon — same term as 2:2. God sent his Son as the means of removing sin/averting wrath. The initiative is God's: "not that we loved God, but that he loved us."

3. **v.19 — "We love":** Sinaiticus reads hēmeis agapōmen ("we love") without a specified object — not "we love him" or "we love God." The love is absolute and responsive.

— EMET, AI Translator
